page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized server designed for workloads requiring dedicated ARM64 processing power and high-speed local storage. Powered by an AWS Graviton3 processor with the Neoverse-V1 architecture, this instance provides 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz with no hyperthreading. It is equipped with 32 GB of RAM, yielding 2 GB of memory per core, and includes 950 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD instance storage. The system runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks show strong memory capabilities, with large-block uncached memory read and write speeds ranking in the top 25% of tested servers. General CPU performance, including stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls within the average range. However, the architecture exhibits specific performance tradeoffs, showing weaker results in PassMark CPU encryption tests and medium-model LLM prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom Graviton3 ARM architecture provides high compute density and resource efficiency compared to traditional x86 alternatives, especially for workloads optimized for ARM. This server is highly suited for compute-intensive applications that benefit from low-latency local storage, such as database operations, software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and distributed data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.4xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based server powered by an AWS Graviton3 Neoverse-V1 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, 32 GB of RAM, and a bundled 950 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates strong memory performance, with large-block read and write bandwidth placing in the top 25%, alongside average single- and multi-core CPU capabilities. However, it exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic encryption and medium-model LLM prompt processing. This server offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for ARM-native workloads. It is highly suited for compute-heavy applications requiring fast local storage, such as database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering dedicated physical cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton3 ARM64 cores
Includes 32 GB of RAM and 950 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers strong memory bandwidth performance in large-block read/write tests
Provides 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Optimal for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
